activities_projects.title: Interpretation of the Poem’s Title
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the literal and metaphorical meanings of the title
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the literal meaning of the word 'concrete'
Discuss the symbolic meaning of the title in relation to Mrs. Jones’s garden
Write a paragraph explaining how the title relates to the theme of the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop deeper literary interpretation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and depth of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis of Mrs. Jones
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate Mrs. Jones’s personality through textual evidence
steps: Read the poem and identify lines that describe Mrs. Jones’s garden
Analyse how these details reflect her personality and interests
Write a short character sketch of Mrs. Jones using evidence from the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to interpret character traits from literary descriptions
activities_projects.evaluation: Use of evidence and clarity of analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Imagery and Visualisation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand how imagery helps readers visualise scenes
steps: Identify descriptive phrases in the poem that create imagery
Write them in your notebook
Draw a visual representation of Mrs. Jones’s garden based on the poem
Explain how the imagery influenced your drawing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literary imagery with visual interpretation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and creativity in representation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analysis of Humour and Irony
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how humour and situational irony create meaning in the poem
steps: Identify the humorous moment where the speaker stands on the flower
Explain why this situation is ironic
Discuss how the poet uses humour to highlight differences in perspective
Write a short explanation in your notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand literary devices such as humour and irony

activities_projects.title: Poetic Device Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Identify and analyse poetic devices used in the poem
steps: Find examples of alliteration and rhyme in the poem
Write them in your notebook
Explain how these devices contribute to the rhythm and tone of the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how poetic devices enhance meaning

activities_projects.title: Comparative Garden Interpretation
activities_projects.objective: Compare different perceptions of beauty in nature
steps: Describe Mrs. Jones’s garden based on the poem
Describe your idea of a beautiful garden
Compare both gardens and discuss the differences
Write a short comparative paragraph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about personal perspectives
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary and Context Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the meaning of words in literary context
steps: Identify descriptive words such as 'peculiar', 'delicate', and 'lovely'
Explain their meanings in the context of the poem
Write sentences using these words in a different context
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of sentence formation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Word Formation Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Understand word formation through letter substitution
steps: Observe the base words provided in the exercise
Replace the first letter to create new meaningful words
Match them with the hints given in the exercise
Explain how the meaning changes with each letter replacement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness of word structure and vocabulary patterns
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct word formation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Listening and Research Task
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem with real-world examples of creative gardens
materials: Presentation on the Rock Garden of Chandigarh
steps: Listen to the presentation about the Rock Garden
Note important facts about its design and features
Compare the Rock Garden with Mrs. Jones’s garden
Write a short reflection
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect literature with real-world examples
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and clarity of reflection (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Poetry Writing
steps: Think about a unique place in nature
Write a short poem describing it
Include imagery, rhyme, and humour similar to the poem
Share your poem with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative writing and poetic expression
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, use of poetic devices, and clarity (10 marks)
